claim_text: This approach captures perturbative noise in the DFD formulation in a way analogous to the noise level estimation methodologies of Luisier et al.

sub_claims.text: luisier2008 introduces or describes a noise level estimation methodology.

evidence.snippet: SURE-LET Multichannel Image Denoising: Interscale Orthonormal Wavelet Thresholding ... the denoising algorithm is parameterized as a linear expansion of thresholds (LET) and optimized using Stein's unbiased risk estimate (SURE). ... Index Terms-Color image denoising, interscale dependencies, multichannel image denoising, nonredundant transforms, orthonormal wavelet transforms (OWTs), Stein's unbiased risk estimate (SURE).
These images are corrupted by an additive channel-wise Gaussian white noise of known interchannel covariance matrix , i.e., ...

Ideally, our aim would be to choose so that it minimizes the MSE defined in (3). A difficulty is that we do not have access to the original noise-free image . Fortunately, we can rely on an adapted version of Stein's unbiased risk estimate (SURE) [28] to accurately estimate this actual MSE, as shown in the following theorem.
(5) is an unbiased estimator of the expected MSE, i.e., ...
In this section, we show how to adapt the monochannel SURE-based denoiser presented in [29] to multichannel image denoising.

evidence.section: III. ALGORITHM / A. Color Image Denoising
evidence.snippet: Color spaces usually consist of channels and we mostly consider red-green-blue (RGB) representations here. In order to demonstrate the performance of our approach, we assume that the interchannel noise covariance matrix is given by ... As an illustration, suppose that we want to perform the denoising in the luminance-chrominance space YUV...

We have proposed an extension of our previous monochannel denoiser to properly handle multichannel images. The resulting interscale-interchannel wavelet estimator consists of a linear expansion of thresholding functions, whose parameters are solved for by minimizing an unbiased estimate of the expected mean squared error between the noise-free signal and the denoised one.

sub_claims.paper_value: SURE-LET denoising: wavelet thresholding parameters optimized by Stein's unbiased risk estimate (SURE) of the MSE; noise covariance assumed known
sub_claims.nuance: Luisier & Blu (2008) present SURE-LET, a wavelet-domain denoising algorithm whose parameters are optimized by Stein's unbiased risk estimate of the MSE; they explicitly treat the interchannel noise covariance matrix as *known* (or 'given') rather than estimated. SURE estimates the risk/MSE, not the noise level. The paper contains no methodology for estimating the noise level/variance from data.

remediation.suggested_edit: Luisier & Blu (2008) describe a wavelet-domain denoising method (SURE-LET) in which the noise covariance is *assumed known*; SURE estimates the MSE/risk, not the noise level. If the manuscript intends to invoke a noise-level (variance) estimator, cite a canonical method such as Donoho & Johnstone's MAD-of-finest-wavelet-subband estimator, or a more recent Luisier et al. SURE-based Poisson-Gaussian noise-parameter estimation paper (e.g., Luisier, Blu, Unser 2011 'Image denoising in mixed Poisson-Gaussian noise'). Alternatively, reword to '...analogous to the SURE-based risk-minimization methodology of Luisier et al.'

phrasings_tried: noise variance estimation / estimate the noise
known noise / noise covariance / assumed noise statistics
MAD / median absolute deviation / Donoho-style noise estimator

section_checklist.section: III. ALGORITHM (A. Interscale Predictor, B. New Interscale-Interchannel Thresholding Function)
IV. Results (A. Color Image Denoising, B. Multiband Image Denoising, Portilla BLS-GSM comparison)

attestation.closest_adjacent: The closest passages are (a) the SURE theorem (Section II, around L15–L16) which gives an unbiased *MSE* estimator — a risk estimator, not a noise-level estimator — and (b) the explicit statement in Section II (L9) that the noise has 'known interchannel covariance matrix' and Section III.A (L34) 'we assume that the interchannel noise covariance matrix is given'. These show the paper *consumes* a known noise level rather than *producing* one.
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: The paper does not credit another primary for a noise-level estimation methodology; it simply does not perform noise-level estimation. Its SURE machinery cites Stein [28] for the risk-estimation principle and prior Luisier work [29] for the monochannel SURE-LET denoiser.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript invokes Luisier et al. for 'noise level estimation'; the cited paper's methodology is *denoising* (wavelet thresholding parameter optimization) via SURE, assuming known noise statistics. This is a context/scope mismatch — SURE estimates the expected squared error, not the noise level. If the citation is meant to support a risk-estimation analogy, the wording in the manuscript should be updated; if it is meant to support noise-level estimation specifically, a different reference is needed.
